authorization.reference: Operator authorized Phase 0 planning, requested WP-FF-008 through WP-FF-011 as detailed no-context STUB packets linked to the canonical design and readiness topics, and required the stubs to be committed and pushed before WP-FF-003 implementation begins.

blockers: WP-FF-003 executable gate bootstrap must be VALIDATED before this compiled spike starts.
WP-FF-005 process/error/diagnostic contracts and WP-FF-009 compound-resource/cancellation models must be stable enough to consume.
Windows and Unix CI or equivalent reproducible validation hosts must be available for descendant-process proof.
lifecycle.next_action: After prerequisites pass, refresh official FFmpeg and current Rust/OS process-supervision research, freeze supported host and FFmpeg identity profiles, promote the STUB, then implement deterministic fake-child and real FFmpeg/ffprobe containment tests on Windows and Unix.

scope.summary: Build a bounded Phase 0 supervisor prototype proving that required FFmpeg and ffprobe external processes can be selected, invoked, observed, cancelled, fully reaped, and output-validated on Windows and Unix without a shell or hidden unbounded pipes.
in_scope: Define trusted absolute FFmpeg/ffprobe executable selection, identity binding, version/capability probing, cache invalidation, and typed missing/wrong-version/capability errors.
Define typed operation plans and direct argument vectors with explicit stream maps and stream-copy selection; arbitrary shell command construction is forbidden.
Scrub inherited environment, govern working directory, pass -nostdin, restrict protocols/input mechanisms, and treat stderr/progress/output as untrusted bounded inputs.
Implement the Spawned, Running, GracefulStopRequested, ForcedKillRequested, Reaped, and Validated supervisor state model with a terminal reap on every path.
Attach Unix process groups or Windows Job Objects at creation and prove bounded graceful stop, forced descendant-tree termination, orphan detection, and cancellation races.
Drain media/stdout, framed progress, and stderr concurrently with item/byte bounds, bounded parser work, bounded diagnostic tail, and explicit chatty-child handling.
Account for FFmpeg process slots, CPU threads, memory, disk I/O, and pipe bytes through the resource model; distinguish mux/stream-copy and transcode admission classes.
Validate successful outputs with ffprobe-normalized facts before commit/archive eligibility and reject crashed, partial, wrong-stream, malformed, or unprobed outputs.
Run deterministic fake-child cases plus basic real FFmpeg discovery, stream-copy, progress, cancellation, reaping, and ffprobe validation on Windows and Unix.

Produce machine-readable reports with executable identities, argument-plan hash, lifecycle transitions, limits, exit/reap result, descendant result, output validation, and residual uncertainty without exposing sensitive paths or inputs.
non_goals: Implementing every post-processing operation, codec, container, metadata, subtitle, thumbnail, chapter, transcode, or live-mux feature.
Shipping or silently downloading an FFmpeg bundle, adding a shell, or adding another runtime/native dependency without Operator approval.
Treating process exit code alone as successful output validation.
Claiming all possible child hangs or all platform process failures can be detected.
Claiming release performance, complete FFmpeg compatibility, or broad implementation readiness from the spike.
dependencies: WP-FF-005-versioned-core-contracts-v1 accepted process, error, cancellation, event, and diagnostic contracts
WP-FF-009-resource-durability-models-v1 accepted compound admission and cancellation models
Required external FFmpeg and ffprobe test identities available on Windows and Unix hosts
Canonical architecture, PR, and triggered deep gates implemented by fforager-xtask

acceptance_matrix.requirement: A versioned process contract defines trusted executable identity/capability selection, typed operation plans, direct argument vectors, scrubbed environment, working directory, protocol/input allowlist, resource vector, deadlines, lifecycle transitions, cancellation, reaping, diagnostics, and ffprobe validation.

Windows tests attach a Job Object at child creation and Unix tests establish a process group; bounded graceful and forced cancellation leave no surviving descendant in the declared test matrix, and every terminal path awaits and records reaping.

Bounded concurrent draining handles chatty, blocked, malformed, reordered, truncated, and stalled progress/stderr/stdout without deadlock, unbounded memory, silent required-data loss, or parser work beyond declared limits.

Mandatory cases cover missing, wrong-version, capability-mismatch, hung, chatty, crashed, cancelled, partial-output, invalid-output, stale capability cache, path substitution, and descendant-process behavior on Windows and Unix with typed failure oracles.

Basic real-tool discovery, stream-copy with explicit maps and -c copy, machine-readable progress, cancellation, reaping, and ffprobe-normalized output validation pass on pinned test identities; success is impossible before Reaped and Validated.

Machine-readable reports bind source, host, executable/version/capability identity, plan, limits, lifecycle, exit/reap/descendant results, output facts, fixture hashes, timeouts, and residual uncertainty without leaking sensitive input values.
FF-GATE-ARCH-001, FF-GATE-PR-001, triggered process/fault/platform deep gates, and independent adversarial review pass for the same source and fixture/tool manifest.

checks_run: git status --porcelain=v2 --branch: main at 447b4dbb0d64df56b3c956d8f311ff24f5b51c50 before packet creation
git worktree list --porcelain: one main worktree; no packet worktree created
blockers: Packet is a STUB and does not authorize implementation.
last_checkpoint.next_action: Wait for prerequisites and cross-platform proof hosts, then refresh research and promote before prototype code.

not_proven: Windows Job Object or Unix process-group descendant containment
Complete post-processing support or broad readiness

repository_layout.runtime_boundary: Shipped product execution must require neither .GOV/ nor build/.

promotion_requirements: WP-FF-003, WP-FF-005, and WP-FF-009 prerequisite evidence is accepted.
Current official FFmpeg documentation and current Rust/Windows/Unix process-supervision implementations are inspected at source and test level.
Supported host, FFmpeg/ffprobe identity, capability, and fixture profiles are pinned.
Deterministic fake-child behaviors and real-tool cases have exact deadlines, limits, failure oracles, and report schema.
Windows and Unix proof execution is available; one platform cannot stand in for both.

documentation_topics.heading: FFmpeg and Post-Processing Integration
documentation_topics.use: complete supervised-process contract and mandatory cases

readiness_matrix_items.required_resolution: Trusted selection, capability/version, restrictions, bounded pipes, containment, cancellation, reaping, and ffprobe validation.
Compound process, CPU, memory, disk, and pipe admission; stricter transcode admission.
Trusted absolute FFmpeg/ffprobe paths or an Operator-chosen verified bundle.

readiness_matrix_items.gate: FFmpeg supervision failure and descendant-process tests on Windows and Unix

base_scope: Bounded direct invocation and pipe handling
ffprobe output validation and machine-readable evidence

high_roi_additions.addition: Create a small deterministic Rust fake-child test executable with modes for chatty pipes, malformed progress, grandchildren, ignored graceful stop, delayed exit, partial output, and exit races.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The supervisor already needs all these paths and a fake child makes them fast, offline, deterministic, and reusable for plugin/worker supervision.
high_roi_additions.gap_closed: Real FFmpeg cannot reliably produce every failure schedule.
high_roi_additions.validation: Each mode has a golden lifecycle report and a mutation that must fail.

high_roi_additions.addition: Bind capability-cache entries to executable file identity plus version/capability probe and invalidate on identity change.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Identity is already collected, and this prevents stale or substituted-tool execution without another subsystem.
high_roi_additions.gap_closed: A path-stable binary replacement could otherwise reuse incorrect capabilities.
high_roi_additions.validation: Replace the test executable at the same path and require a re-probe.

high_roi_additions.addition: Expose one stable supervisor timeline schema usable by the watcher and no-context diagnostics without making watcher acknowledgement part of correctness.
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Lifecycle instrumentation is being built now and will make Phase 2 failures much cheaper to isolate.
high_roi_additions.gap_closed: Process-tree and pipe stalls are otherwise opaque.
high_roi_additions.validation: Replay covers success, hung, killed, reaped, and invalid-output timelines; product behavior is identical with watcher absent.

reused_systems: WP-FF-003 executable gate and tooling policy
WP-FF-009 resource vectors, cancellation, and controlled clock
Required external FFmpeg and ffprobe binaries
gaps_closed: Pipe deadlock and unbounded stderr/progress memory
Terminal paths that fail to reap
Partial or wrong output accepted from exit code alone

red_team.risk: A child or descendant survives cancellation.
mitigations: bounded graceful then forced tree kill
await reaping on every terminal path
red_team.verification: Fake-child descendant matrix on Windows and Unix plus post-exit orphan scan.

red_team.risk: Bounded progress/stderr handling deadlocks media output.
red_team.verification: Chatty/malformed/stalled fake-child cases stay within memory/time bounds and terminate.

red_team.risk: A substituted binary inherits trusted cached capabilities.
failure_scenarios: version output spoofed without file identity
red_team.verification: Same-path replacement and link-target mutation force rejection or re-probe.

red_team.risk: Successful exit masks partial or semantically wrong output.
red_team.verification: Partial/wrong/stale artifacts never reach Validated or archive eligibility.

red_team.risk: Cross-platform evidence is inferred from one OS.
failure_scenarios: Unix group logic assumed on Windows
Windows Job behavior assumed under Unix
mitigations: mandatory Windows and Unix report rows
red_team.verification: Closure aggregator rejects a missing platform report or mismatched source/tool manifest.

required_proof_classes: Contract schemas and typed plan fixtures
Deterministic fake-child boundary and negative cases
Real FFmpeg/ffprobe smoke and stream-copy validation
verification_needs.closure_rule: No PASS without both Windows and Unix descendant/reaping reports for the same source contract, bounded pipe evidence, and ffprobe validation; exit code or process disappearance alone is insufficient.
